summary:
The anterior two-thirds of the tongue receive their primary sensory innervation from the lingual nerve, branching off as part of V3; no other major nerves predominantly supply these regions based on current medical literature. Agent1 concluded after reviewing multiple sources that the anterior two-thirds of the tongue get its main sensory input via the lingual nerve originating from the third cranial nerve’s lower jaw component. No conflicting information was found regarding alternative predominant suppliers for those areas mentioned within reviewed materials. To summarize concisely:

The anterior two-thirds of the tongue are mainly supplied by the lingual nerve derived from the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve (V3), providing essential sensations without significant contributions from different or additional pathways noted across reliable resources consulted here today. This summary captures both core aspects—location specificity concerning anatomical distribution—and emphasizes reliance solely upon one specific neural pathway while ruling out others through No conflicting information was found regarding alternative predominant suppliers for those areas mentioned within reviewed materials. To summarize concisely:
